request.question: Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles HeLa intracellular drug accumulation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.

plan.objective: To determine the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be treated with either folate-conjugated PLGA nanoparticles loaded with doxorubicin or free doxorubicin. The intracellular drug accumulation will be quantified using fluorescence spectroscopy after a defined incubation period. The experiment will include multiple replicates for statistical validity.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 10 µM.
Untreated HeLa cells without any drug treatment.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Dissolve 100 mg of PLGA (Resomer RG 503H, Evonik) in 5 mL of dichloromethane (DCM) and add 10 mg of doxorubicin (doxorubicin hydrochloride, Sigma-Aldrich) to the solution. Stir for 30 min at room temperature to ensure complete dissolution.
Equipment: Magnetic stirrer, 50 mL round-bottom flask
Reagents: 100 mg PLGA (Resomer RG 503H), 10 mg doxorubicin hydrochloride, 5 mL dichloromethane (DCM)
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin is fully dissolved in the PLGA solution.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 20 mL of 1% w/v polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution to the PLGA-doxorubicin mixture and emulsify using a probe sonicator (Sonics & Materials, VCX 130) at 40% amplitude for 2 min with 10 s intervals to prevent overheating.
Equipment: Sonicator (Sonics & Materials, VCX 130), 50 mL beaker
Reagents: 20 mL 1% w/v PVA solution
Expected outcome: Emulsion is formed with uniform droplet size.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 4 h 15 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Allow the emulsion to stir at room temperature for 4 h to evaporate DCM. Then, centrifuge the solution at 10,000 × g for 15 min at 4°C to collect the nanoparticles.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 50 mL conical tubes
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles are pelleted at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle Characterization
Resuspend the nanoparticle pellet in 1 mL of phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and analyze the size and zeta potential using a Malvern Zetasizer Nano ZS at 25°C.
Equipment: Malvern Zetasizer Nano ZS
Reagents: 1 mL PBS
Expected outcome: Nanoparticle size is approximately 200 nm with a zeta potential of -30 mV.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in 6-well plates at a density of 1 × 10^5 cells/well in 2 mL of DMEM supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin. Incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 h.
Equipment: T75 cell culture flasks, 6-well plates, CO2 incubator
Reagents: DMEM, 10% FBS, 1% penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: Cells reach 70-80% confluency.

DAY 1 | 4 h | Drug Treatment
Treat HeLa cells with either 10 µM free doxorubicin or 10 µM doxorubicin-loaded folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in 2 mL of DMEM. Incubate for 4 h at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Equipment: CO2 incubator
Reagents: 10 µM free doxorubicin, 10 µM doxorubicin-loaded folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
Expected outcome: Cells are treated with specified drug formulations.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Drug Accumulation Measurement
Wash cells twice with PBS and lyse with 500 µL of lysis buffer (1% Triton X-100 in PBS). Measure fluorescence intensity at 480 nm excitation and 590 nm emission using a BioTek Synergy H1 microplate reader.
Equipment: BioTek Synergy H1 microplate reader
Reagents: PBS, 1% Triton X-100 lysis buffer
Expected outcome: Fluorescence intensity is quantified for each treatment group.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin by normalizing fluorescence intensity to protein concentration measured via BCA assay. Compare the accumulation between free doxorubicin and nanoparticle formulations using a two-tailed t-test with a significance level of p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined, confirming at least 3-fold higher accumulation in nanoparticle-treated cells.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells allowing for enhanced uptake of folate-conjugated nanoparticles.
Doxorubicin retains its bioactivity when conjugated to PLGA nanoparticles.
The fluorescence intensity of doxorubicin correlates with its intracellular concentration.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intracellular doxorubicin accumulation in nanoparticle-treated cells is at least 3-fold higher than in free doxorubicin-treated cells.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of PLGA and doxorubicin for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (less than 10 passages).
Calibrate the microplate reader before use.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
